title: heylandia perrottetii
intro: Heylandia perrottetii, also known as the Indian Spurge or Pichka, is a plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and analgesic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, providing relief from pain and inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Heylandia perrottetii, including extracts and infusions, are used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine, often in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic benefits.
